action: Authenticate peer-trigger Merkle checkpoints and detect equivocation across witness observations.

progress.finding: Signed checkpoint statements authenticate key possession; retained witness comparisons expose equal-size divergent roots, while RFC 9162 consistency proofs bind valid unequal-size extensions.
progress.verification: Implementation and adversarial tests are committed. Local exact-file execution was blocked by DNS resolution failure and provider CI was not observed, so no passing-test claim is made.
progress.limits: Ed25519 verifies key possession, not signer authorization, key custody, freshness, witness independence, quorum sufficiency, payload truth, or global non-equivocation.

handoff: A future Frontier slice should add exact-commit CI and a deny-by-default multi-witness quorum policy with signer-key rotation, revocation, freshness, and duplicate-witness controls.
